values.content:
=# Role You are an expert academic note organizer and document formatter. Transform raw, unstructured notes into clean, structured, highly readable HTML documents suitable for study and revision. Raw data: {{ $json.content }} # Task - Correct grammar and spelling without altering meaning. - Organize into logical hierarchy: Main topics as

, Subtopics as

, Supporting sections as

. - Group related ideas into clear paragraphs using

. - Convert lists into properly structured bullet points using

    and
  • . - Emphasize key terms using . - Do NOT add new information or external knowledge. # Output - Output ONLY valid HTML (no markdown, no JSON, no explanations). - Include a minimal inline

=# Role
You are a metadata extractor. You receive a formatted HTML document of academic notes.

# Task
Extract the following fields from the content:
- title: The main topic or subject of the notes (short, 3-6 words)
- subject: The academic subject/course (e.g. Biology, History, Physics)
- date: Today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format (use {{ $now.toFormat('yyyy-MM-dd') }})
- summary: One sentence describing what the notes cover

# Output
Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object. No markdown, no explanation.

Example:
{
  "title": "Cell Division and Mitosis",
  "subject": "Biology",
  "date": "2024-04-18",
  "summary": "Notes covering the stages of mitosis and cell cycle regulation."
}

HTML Input:{{ $json.output[0].content[0].text }}

json.content:
Cloud platforms
A cloud platform is a complete environment that provides computing resources tool services and infrastructure over the internet to build deploy manage applications
Components of a cloud platform
Compute - virtual machine or containers
Storage - database
Network- load balancing or routing
Tools - API ide 
Security - iam identity access management
Monitoring - logs and analytic
Example of cloud platform
Amazon aws - Amazon web service
Microsoft azure
Google cloud platform
Step by step working of cloud platforms 
User send a request for the services to the cloud platform provided by service provide through internet 
Cloud platforms processes the request based on cloud resources available
Data stored or retrieved from the cloud storage depending upon the request of the user
The response to the request is delivered to the user instantly depending upon the availability of the cloud resource
Impact of cloud platforms
Impact on business
No capex is required to procure hardware
Cloud platforms follow pay as you go model
Deployment of the apps can be done very quickly
The resources or instances of resources can be increased or decreased at any time based on requirement of the business
Impact on education
Virtual labs
Online learning platforms
Remote access to software

content.text:
```html

Cloud Platforms

A cloud platform is a complete environment that provides computing resources, tool services, and infrastructure over the internet to build, deploy, and manage applications.

Components of a Cloud Platform

  • Compute: Virtual machines or containers
  • Storage: Databases
  • Network: Load balancing or routing
  • Tools: API, IDE
  • Security: IAM (Identity Access Management)
  • Monitoring: Logs and analytics

Examples of Cloud Platforms

  • Amazon AWS (Amazon Web Services)
  • Microsoft Azure
  • Google Cloud Platform

Step-by-Step Working of Cloud Platforms

  1. User sends a request for services to the cloud platform provided by the service provider through the internet.
  2. The cloud platform processes the request based on the cloud resources available.
  3. Data is stored or retrieved from the cloud storage depending upon the user's request.
  4. The response to the request is delivered to the user instantly depending upon the availability of the cloud resource.

Impact of Cloud Platforms

Impact on Business

  • No capital expenditure (CAPEX) is required to procure hardware.
  • Cloud platforms follow a pay-as-you-go model.
  • Deployment of applications can be done very quickly.
  • The resources or instances of resources can be increased or decreased at any time based on the requirements of the business.

Impact on Education

  • Virtual labs
  • Online learning platforms
  • Remote access to software
```

json.htmlContent:
```html

Cloud Platforms

A cloud platform is a complete environment that provides computing resources, tool services, and infrastructure over the internet to build, deploy, and manage applications.

Components of a Cloud Platform

  • Compute: Virtual machines or containers
  • Storage: Databases
  • Network: Load balancing or routing
  • Tools: API, IDE
  • Security: IAM (Identity Access Management)
  • Monitoring: Logs and analytics

Examples of Cloud Platforms

  • Amazon AWS (Amazon Web Services)
  • Microsoft Azure
  • Google Cloud Platform

Step-by-Step Working of Cloud Platforms

  1. User sends a request for services to the cloud platform provided by the service provider through the internet.
  2. The cloud platform processes the request based on the cloud resources available.
  3. Data is stored or retrieved from the cloud storage depending upon the user's request.
  4. The response to the request is delivered to the user instantly depending upon the availability of the cloud resource.

Impact of Cloud Platforms

Impact on Business

  • No capital expenditure (CAPEX) is required to procure hardware.
  • Cloud platforms follow a pay-as-you-go model.
  • Deployment of applications can be done very quickly.
  • The resources or instances of resources can be increased or decreased at any time based on the requirements of the business.

Impact on Education

  • Virtual labs
  • Online learning platforms
  • Remote access to software
```
